review_comment:
1. It's good to think this through, but TBH it's not clear to me that we need to introduce further distinctions between interactive and static rendering on this behavior. Unless there's a known use case, that is.
2. That's a great thing to anticipate. I think the only reason for subclassing `NavigationException` is if you're also implementing a custom rendering platform, and you want a way to extend the meaning of "navigation" on your platform with other fields/flags (and in that case, you don't want it to trigger an error boundary - it would just be an obscure bug you probably didn't realise you caused). People can't really subclass it for arbitrary non-navigation-related reasons because the framework is still going to catch their subclass and use it to perform a navigation, hence it seems unlikely the feature could be misused.

So in summary my guess is your existing design/implementation is already the best design. Unless you know of more reasons we should anticipate misuse/problems.

> So in summary my guess is your existing design/implementation is already the best design. Unless you know of more reasons we should anticipate misuse/problems.

Yeah, I agree - I don't anticipate that either of the points I brought up will become real problems, but I figured it might be a good idea to bring them up in case anyone thought otherwise. There's room to revise this in the future if we feel the need to as well.
